mission.title: kubeflow: Admission webhook doesn't pick up new cert when it gets renewed by cert-manager
mission.description: Admission webhook doesn't pick up new cert when it gets renewed by cert-manager. Users encounter: "tls: bad certificate".

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `tls: bad certificate`

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**
Admission webhook keeps throwing the below error every once in a while:
http: TLS handshake error from *****:34006: remote error: tls: bad certificate
This causes pod defaults not to work.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Admission webhook doesn't pick up new cert when it gets
steps.description:
I have created this PR as a quick way to update the Kubeflow Roadmap to include KF 1.6.   My view is that we can update this in a simple way and then add more details if needed.     Please review, improve as you see needed.   Thanks.   Josh

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Admission webhook doesn't pick up new cert when is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `tls: bad certificate` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: I have created this PR as a quick way to update the Kubeflow Roadmap to include KF 1.6.   My view is that we can update this in a simple way and then add more details if needed.     Please review, improve as you see needed.   Thanks.   Josh

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
